question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.

internal_assessment.choice_policy: 33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.
internal_assessment.chapter_weightage_note: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.

class_xi_projects_suggested: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of the methods of purification of water

Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water and study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit (if any)
Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
class_xii_projects_suggested: Study of the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study of quantity of casein present in different samples of milk
Preparation of soybean milk and its comparison with the natural milk with respect to curd formation, effect of temperature, etc.
Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time, etc.)
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of following materials: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom)
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Fundamental Chemical Concepts and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Ability to understand the nature of matter, apply laws of chemical combination, perform stoichiometric calculations with uncertainty analysis, and represent phenomena at macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic levels.
competencies: Apply Dalton's Atomic Theory and Mole Concept for quantitative analysis
Perform Stoichiometric Calculations involving percentage composition and molar masses
Measure physical quantities with appropriate precision and analyze uncertainty

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Ability to trace the development from Bohr's model to the quantum mechanical model, understand sub-atomic particles, and apply quantum numbers.
competencies: Explain the discovery of sub-atomic particles and atomic models
Apply Bohr's Model for Hydrogen Atom calculations
Interpret Quantum Mechanical Model concepts including orbitals

competency_groups.title: Periodicity and Classification
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify elements based on electronic configuration, understand periodic trends across periods and groups, and apply the Modern Periodic Law.
competencies: Classify s-, p-, d-, f- block elements using electronic configurations
Predict properties of elements based on their position in the periodic table
Analyze periodic trends such as atomic radius and ionization enthalpy
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure
competency_groups.description: Ability to predict molecular geometry, bond types, and stability using various bonding theories including VSEPR, Valence Bond Theory, and MO Theory.
competencies: Apply Kossel-Lewis approach for ionic and covalent bonds
Predict molecular shapes using VSEPR theory
Explain hybridization concepts and Molecular Orbital Theory applications

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics and Energy Changes
competency_groups.description: Ability to analyze energy changes in chemical reactions, calculate enthalpy variations, determine spontaneity using Gibbs energy, and understand equilibrium conditions.
competencies: Calculate Enthalpy Change (∆H) for various reaction types via calorimetry
Determine Spontaneity of processes using Gibbs Energy Change

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium Principles and Applications
competency_groups.description: Ability to analyze dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes, apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium, and understand factors affecting ionic equilibria.
competencies: Apply Le Chatelier's principle to predict shifts in equilibrium

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes
competency_groups.description: Ability to identify oxidation-reduction processes, balance redox equations using electron transfer concepts, and understand electrode reactions.
competencies: Identify oxidizing and reducing agents based on oxidation numbers

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms
competency_groups.description: Ability to represent organic structures, apply IUPAC nomenclature, understand isomerism, analyze reaction mechanisms, and perform purification techniques.
competencies: Apply structural representations and classification of organic compounds
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Reactivity
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatic hydrocarbons, analyze their physical properties, and understand carcinogenic nature.
competencies: Differentiate between saturated and unsaturated compounds

competency_groups.title: Solutions and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Ability to express solution concentrations, analyze vapor pressure relationships in ideal/non-ideal solutions, and determine molar masses using colligative properties.
competencies: Calculate concentration expressions for various types of solutions

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry and Cell Potential
competency_groups.description: Ability to distinguish between galvanic and electrolytic cells, apply the Nernst equation for cell potential calculations, and understand corrosion mechanisms.
competencies: Analyze electrochemical cells using standard electrode potentials

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics and Reaction Rates
competency_groups.description: Ability to determine reaction rates, apply integrated rate equations, analyze temperature dependence using collision theory, and understand factors influencing kinetics.
competencies: Apply Collision Theory of Chemical Reactions

competency_groups.title: d- and f-Block Elements Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Ability to describe electronic configurations of transition elements, explain general properties including oxidation states, and understand lanthanoid/actinoid characteristics.
competencies: Explain the position of d-block elements in the periodic table

competency_groups.title: Coordination Chemistry and Bonding
competency_groups.description: Ability to apply Werner's theory, use IUPAC nomenclature for coordination compounds, analyze isomerism types, and understand bonding in metal carbonyls.
competencies: Apply Nomenclature rules for Coordination Compounds

competency_groups.title: Organic Halogen Derivatives and Functional Groups
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify haloalkanes/haloarenes, predict preparation methods, analyze physical properties, and understand chemical reactions including polyhalogen compounds.
competencies: Analyze the nature of C-X bond in halogen derivatives

competency_groups.title: Alcohols, Phenols, Ethers, Aldehydes, Ketones, Acids, Amines
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify organic functional groups, apply nomenclature rules for alcohols, phenols, ethers, carbonyl compounds, and amines; analyze their preparation methods and chemical reactivity.
competencies: Differentiate structures of Functional Groups in Alcohols, Phenols, Ethers

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules and Biochemical Processes
competency_groups.description: Ability to classify carbohydrates, proteins, vitamins, nucleic acids, and hormones; understand their structures and biological functions.
competencies: Classify Biomolecules including Carbohydrates, Proteins, Enzymes

competency_groups.title: Laboratory Techniques and Analytical Skills
competency_groups.description: Ability to perform basic laboratory techniques, characterize substances via melting/boiling points, purify compounds through crystallization, determine pH, and conduct quantitative analysis.
competencies: Perform Characterization of Chemical Substances using physical constants
competency_groups.chapter_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

competency_groups.title: Scientific Inquiry and Problem Solving
competency_groups.description: Ability to design investigatory projects, analyze data from experiments (e.g., water quality, food adulteration), apply problem-solving skills in real-life contexts, and integrate life skills.
competencies: Design Scientific Investigations involving laboratory testing

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations.

internal_assessment.choice_policy: 33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.
internal_assessment.chapter_weightage_note: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.

class_xi_projects_suggested: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of the methods of purification of water

Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water and study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit (if any)
Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
class_xii_projects_suggested: Study of the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening
Study of quantity of casein present in different samples of milk
Preparation of soybean milk and its comparison with the natural milk with respect to curd formation, effect of temperature, etc.
Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time, etc.)
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of following materials: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom)
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper
